title: Parliament Debates Most Contractionary Budget in Decades
detail: Debate continues in Iran's Majlis over the draft budget bill for Iranian year 1405, submitted by President Pezeshkian on December 23. Economists describe it as the most contractionary budget in decades, with an overall ceiling increase of less than 5% against inflation above 40%, amounting to a real contraction of roughly 35% in government spending power. The bill proposes only a 20% raise for public sector wages, widely described as 'paltry and insulting' by labor associations given that inflation has eroded purchasing power by more than half.

title: Budget Deficit Estimated at 400-1,800 Trillion Tomans
detail: Analysts estimate Iran's budget deficit ranges between 400 and 1,800 trillion tomans, a staggering range reflecting the uncertainty of government revenue projections. Oil revenue is running 30% below budget forecasts due to sanctions enforcement, while the rial's collapse has inflated the toman-denominated cost of imports and debt servicing. Critics argue the government plans to cover the deficit 'from the people's pockets' through subsidy cuts, currency devaluation, and inflation rather than structural reform.

title: Labor Unions Warn Workers Will Bear Brunt of Fiscal Crisis
detail: Iranian labor and professional associations intensify criticism of the proposed budget, calling the 20% salary increase for government employees and retirees inadequate when inflation exceeds 42%. The Free Workers' Union warns that the budget institutionalizes poverty for the working class by refusing to index wages to inflation. Retired teachers and civil servants gather outside the Majlis in Tehran to demand a higher increase, in one of several small but growing public demonstrations of economic frustration.
